agent_notes: Enfield produced a quality longlist but not 30-35 qualifying venues; Books & Coffee is the binding constraint because no defensible independent bookshop-plus-coffee venue was found in-borough.
Creative Walks use only approved PAINTS safety-list spaces: Trent Park, Grovelands Park and Forty Hall Estate.

Several north-Enfield heritage venues are Tier B because the required same-category backup within 10 minutes is not realistically available; host should confirm all walk-in/group policies before publishing.

venues.venue_name: The Little Green Dragon Ale House
venues.opening_hours: Tue-Thu 17:00-22:00; Fri-Sun 14:00-22:00; closed Mon. Bank holiday/temporary variations possible; host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-ins structurally favoured: venue says it is a pub not a restaurant and only reserves tables under special circumstances.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Small micropub; 8-12 possible only at quieter early evening/weekend afternoon. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.capacity_notes: Tiny, conversation-led micropub with no TV/slot machines; better for 8 than 12.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 14:00-16:30 or Sunday 14:00-16:00 before music/peak demand.
venues.why_fits_paints: A deeply local micropub built around conversation, cask ale and a refusal of screens. The PAINTS hook is strong: Enfield's first micropub, CAMRA-decorated, and more like a living-room salon than a pub crawl stop.
venues.anecdote_story: Opened in 2017 as Enfield's first micropub; named in the orbit of the old Green Dragon pub and run as a community drinking room.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Changing cask ales, craft keg/keykeg beers, real cider, gin, wine, prosecco and snacks.
venues.safety_notes: Winchmore Hill is a residential high-street/rail village setting; attendees should be routed to Winchmore Hill station or Green Lanes buses after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8-10 min walk by local street route; host must verify on Google Maps before publishing.
venues.risks_watchouts: Peak capacity and group seating are the main risks; no kitchen beyond snacks.

venues.walk_in_policy: Walk-ins accepted; official page notes popular live music and says arrive early. Private hire available but not required for ordinary visits.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Public bar and marquee create realistic group options; heads-up call recommended.
venues.capacity_notes: Traditional two-bar pub with garden/outside area, public bar and marquee.
venues.best_time_slot: Thursday 18:00-20:00 or Sunday 15:00 before/around piano session depending event tone.
venues.why_fits_paints: A proper back-street Enfield pub with live music, a piano ritual and a community story. It has more texture than a generic gastropub while still being operationally robust for a matched group.
venues.anecdote_story: McMullen states the pub has been part of its family since 1877; CAMRA notes historic two-bar fittings and a piano-led Sunday tradition.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Locally brewed McMullen ales, world beers, cocktails, pizza van Thu-Sat, quiz and live music.
venues.safety_notes: Residential route; use Enfield Chase/Enfield Town taxi or bus routing after late events.
venues.backup_venue: The Little Green Dragon Ale House
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 25 min walk, so outside PAINTS 10-min rule; no qualifying same-category backup found within 10 min.
venues.risks_watchouts: Because backup is outside 10 min, host should treat this as operationally weaker on peak music nights.

venues.walk_in_policy: Bookings available; ordinary pub walk-ins likely, but group of 8-12 must be confirmed.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable because it is a larger food pub, but UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.capacity_notes: Refurbished country-style pub with garden and unusual on-site petting farm.
venues.why_fits_paints: The miniature farm gives this a very Enfield-specific story and a warmer texture than a standard pub. It is not natural-wine coded, but it does satisfy the character-pub route of Show & Tell Drinks.
venues.anecdote_story: CAMRA and the venue both foreground the garden mini-farm, a rare pub detail that can become the group's shared opening prompt.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Real ales, fine wines, homemade pub food and Sunday roasts.
venues.safety_notes: Lower Edmonton evening routing needs more host care; attendees should leave in pairs or via pre-agreed station/bus route.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying drinks backup found within 10 min - closest PAINTS-coded alternative is The Wonder / Little Green Dragon by transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Tier B due to safety/routing scrutiny and weak backup coverage.

venues.walk_in_policy: Groups of 6 or more need booking in advance, so this is capped at Tier B.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable only with advance booking; not pure walk-in.
venues.capacity_notes: Community pub/gastropub with garden and food service.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday 15:00-17:00 or weekday early evening with booking.
venues.why_fits_paints: Good local warmth and group capacity near Winchmore Hill, useful as a backup texture to the Little Green Dragon. It is less distinctive and less walk-in friendly, so it stays Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: Local community pub run around food, drinks and events; no deeper historical backstory found.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Seasonal pub food, drinks collection, wine and takeaway pizza/wine.
venues.safety_notes: Residential Winchmore Hill route; straightforward station access.
venues.backup_venue: The Little Green Dragon Ale House
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8-10 min walk; host must verify.
venues.risks_watchouts: Booking requirement conflicts with PAINTS default walk-in model.

venues.opening_hours: Market stalls Thu-Sat 09:00-16:00; open-air food court daily per market site.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for a browsing/food-led group if host sets a fixed meeting point.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor historic market place; weather exposure but food court gives dwell time.
venues.why_fits_paints: The market has genuine borough history rather than pop-up gloss: a charter market dating back to 1303. It gives strangers a natural object-based format: each person finds one stall, food item or old-market detail to present.
venues.anecdote_story: The Old Enfield Charitable Trust runs the market for local benefit; council history notes the Queen unveiled a 700th-anniversary plaque in 2003.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Household staples, handmade/specialist goods and international street food.
venues.safety_notes: Central Enfield Town, daytime strongly preferred.
venues.backup_venue: Dugdale Arts Centre / Museum of Enfield
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 5 min walk; cultural/weather fallback rather than same-market backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Stall mix may vary; not all days will feel equally curated.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Forty Hill / Enfield Town bus connection
venues.opening_hours: Monthly farmers' market; recent listings show 10:00-15:00. Farm shop Fri-Sun 11:00-16:00.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 as a market-and-estate wander, but date must match monthly schedule.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor farm/estate setting with producers and food stalls.
venues.best_time_slot: Second Sunday 10:30-13:00, subject to official date confirmation.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Enfield at its best: an organic farm, an old estate, and food producers in one place. It has a tangible maker/producer focus and pairs naturally with Forty Hall's gardens.
venues.anecdote_story: Forty Hall Farm is described as London's only certified organic farm by partner sources, with its monthly market supporting local food systems.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Local produce, street food, plants and farm goods depending market date.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime estate setting; host should manage transport because it is less tube-adjacent.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min by bus/taxi/transit, not a 15-min walk; acceptable only as market-day transit backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Monthly cadence and weather exposure are the main risks.

venues.opening_hours: First Sunday monthly, typically 11:00-16:00; specific date/time must be confirmed from current socials.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for a daytime group; road-based market format.
venues.capacity_notes: Small community-run market with street-food/vintage/community stalls depending month.
venues.why_fits_paints: A small community market has more neighbourly warmth than a large destination market. The Bowes Park setting adds a useful outer-north counterweight.
venues.anecdote_story: The market grew from local trader, shopper and volunteer support and runs on Myddleton Road, named for New River engineer Sir Hugh Myddelton.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fresh producers, cakes, vintage stalls, street food and community stalls depending month.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; Bowes Park/Bounds Green routes are straightforward.
venues.backup_venue: Palmers Green Action Team street markets / Devonshire Square maker events
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min by local bus or rail/tube hop; not a reliable walking backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Borderline district fit: Bowes Park straddles Enfield/Haringey edges; include only if PAINTS accepts south-west Enfield fringe.

venues.venue_name: Palmers Green Action Team Street Markets at Devonshire Square
venues.opening_hours: Regular street/maker markets announced by Palmers Green Action Team; next exact 2026 dates UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable if event date and stall plan confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor public-square market with local artists, makers, food producers and musicians.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday or Sunday 11:00-13:00 when a market is scheduled.
venues.why_fits_paints: A maker-led square activation fits PAINTS better than a generic retail market. It is included as Tier B because the schedule is less reliably published than Enfield Market or Forty Hall.
venues.anecdote_story: Devonshire Square is part of Palmers Green town-centre placemaking, with market events intended to showcase local makers and food producers.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Local crafts, handmade gifts, food, produce and occasional live music.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Palmers Green high-street setting; straightforward station access.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min by transit, outside walking backup rule.
venues.risks_watchouts: Do not publish without confirming the exact market date and stall list.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Forty Hill / Enfield Town bus connection
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access estate/gardens; house may have event/ticket exceptions. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.capacity_notes: Estate walk can use wide paths and outdoor stops; house/cafe capacity should be checked.
venues.why_fits_paints: Approved safety-list estate, Jacobean house, gardens, water features and farm context make this the most PAINTS-complete Enfield walk. Suggested stops: front of Forty Hall, walled/pleasure gardens, lake/water garden, Forty Hall Farm edge.
venues.anecdote_story: Forty Hall is a Grade I listed Jacobean manor linked to Sir Nicholas Rainton, set in historic parkland and medieval water gardens.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Jacobean manor exterior/interiors when open, gardens, historic parkland, farm edge and seasonal market if timed well.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS safety-list approved: Forty Hall Estate. Daytime route only; avoid isolated woodland edges near closing.
venues.backup_venue: Cedar Tree Cafe / Forty Hall house public areas
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min of walk start if meeting at Forty Hall.
venues.risks_watchouts: Cafe and house opening can vary around private events; host must verify.

venues.opening_hours: Enfield parks open Mon-Sat from 08:00 and Sun/bank holidays from 08:30; seasonal closing times vary.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable daytime, with strict route discipline.
venues.capacity_notes: Large country park; use a compact route near Cockfosters/Trent Park cafe rather than deep woodland.
venues.why_fits_paints: Approved safety-list park with unusually rich story: aristocratic estate, wartime intelligence history and a new museum opening later in July 2026. Suggested stops: Cockfosters entrance, cafe/clearing, mansion approach, lake/woodland edge.
venues.anecdote_story: Trent Park House was used for secret wartime surveillance of German officers; Trent Park House of Secrets opens to the public on 21 July 2026.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Landscape, mansion exterior/context, woodland, lake and historic espionage story.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS safety-list approved: Trent Park. Daytime only; avoid extended isolated woodland and finish before dusk.
venues.backup_venue: Trent Park House of Secrets cafe
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min if meeting near mansion/cafe area.
venues.risks_watchouts: Museum itself is not open until 21 July 2026; do not sell this as a museum visit before then.

venues.opening_hours: Enfield parks open Mon-Sat from 08:00 and Sun/bank holidays from 08:30; seasonal closing times vary.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable daytime, but host should keep to main lake/path loop.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic designed landscape with lake and broad paths; limited wet-weather cultural backup.
venues.why_fits_paints: Safety-list approved and visually strong, with John Nash/estate-garden history. Suggested stops: lake edge, Grovelands House view, Repton/Nash landscape discussion point, main path return.
venues.anecdote_story: The former Southgate Grove mansion was designed by John Nash and the grounds landscaped by Humphry Repton; the park later became public land.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Designed landscape, lake, mansion exterior context and mature tree routes.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS safety-list approved: Grovelands Park. Daytime only; avoid lingering after dusk because of limited enclosed backup options.
venues.backup_venue: No strong weather backup within 5 min - closest practical fallback is a Southgate/Winchmore Hill cafe or pub requiring host verification.
venues.risks_watchouts: Tier B because the weather-backup rule is weak here.

venues.venue_name: Myddelton House Gardens and Bowles Museum
venues.neighbourhood_station: Bulls Cross / Turkey Street bus connection
venues.walk_in_policy: Free open-access garden/museum; no booking indicated. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm group tolerance.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 if kept outdoors and cafe seating is not assumed.
venues.capacity_notes: Eight-acre botanical garden with tea room and small museum display.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is a garden-as-gallery pick: botanical design, glasshouse detail, salvaged artefacts and Bowles' paintings make it more than a pretty walk. The story density is excellent for Show & Tell-style prompts.
venues.anecdote_story: Edward Augustus Bowles, the Crocus King, developed the gardens as a self-taught horticulturist; the site displays his life, garden history and paintings.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Botanical garden, Victorian glasshouse, Bowles Museum, paintings, New River Lawn, Market Cross and rock garden restoration.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime garden setting; less transit-rich, so host should specify arrival/departure route.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 20 min walk / short taxi; no same-category backup within 10 min.
venues.risks_watchouts: Backup rule not met; include only with transport contingency.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Forty Hill / Enfield Town bus connection
venues.walk_in_policy: General visit appears walk-in, but house closures/private events possible; UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for estate and garden; house/cafe group tolerance must be confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Jacobean manor, museum/garden site and cafe.
venues.why_fits_paints: Strong heritage setting and house/garden material, but without a current exhibition strong enough to make it Tier A under the museum rule. Use it as a history-and-looking day, not a blockbuster-gallery day.
venues.anecdote_story: Grade I listed Jacobean manor associated with Sir Nicholas Rainton and the Parker-Bowles family.
venues.serve_or_on_view: House displays, gardens, historic parkland and estate landscape.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime estate visit; host-managed transport recommended.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 20 min walk / short taxi; no qualifying same-category backup within 10 min.
venues.risks_watchouts: Tier B due to weak current-exhibition evidence and no 10-min same-category backup.

venues.opening_hours: Mar-Oct daily 10:00-17:30, last admission 16:00; winter Mon-Fri 10:00-17:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: General admission can be purchased at reception; host must confirm group handling.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 in gardens; cafe/table assumptions UNVERIFIED.
venues.capacity_notes: 30-acre garden campus with many designed garden rooms and cafe.
venues.why_fits_paints: Best treated as a living design/craft museum: garden rooms, horticultural training and plant theatre. It is less culturally sharp than Myddelton House, but operationally easier.
venues.anecdote_story: Capel Manor is tied to Capel Manor College, giving the gardens a working horticulture-education story rather than purely ornamental heritage.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Over 40 garden areas, maze, mini zoo/creatures, planting displays and cafe.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; car/bus arrival needs clear host instructions.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 20 min walk / short taxi; no qualifying 10-min same-category backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Some visitor sentiment flags maintenance/value concerns; host should preview close to event date.

venues.opening_hours: Tuesdays 10:00-16:00, last entry 15:00; last Sunday of the month with exceptions.
venues.walk_in_policy: Museum visit likely walk-in during open hours; group policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible but narrow due to limited opening pattern; host must call.
venues.capacity_notes: Volunteer-run transport museum; allow 2-3 hours per museum guidance.
venues.best_time_slot: Tuesday 10:30-12:30 or last Sunday when confirmed open.
venues.why_fits_paints: Quirky, local and object-rich, but not painting/sculpture/textile/craft-led enough to be a natural PAINTS museum pick. It remains useful as an off-grid B option for groups that like machines, memory and local collections.
venues.anecdote_story: Volunteer-run museum housed at Whitewebbs with transport collections and enthusiast events.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Historic transport collections, vehicles and associated displays.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; less transit-rich and near parkland/roads, so host should manage arrival and departure.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 30+ min walk / short taxi; no qualifying 10-min backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Limited opening days and category fit are the main weaknesses.

venues.venue_name: Dugdale Arts Centre / Museum of Enfield
venues.opening_hours: Museum/gallery hours not fully surfaced in current source; Dugdale programme and cafe hours must be confirmed. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Museum/gallery likely walk-in when open; events/ticketed performances separate. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Operationally easy central location, but exhibition strength varies.
venues.capacity_notes: Arts centre with Museum of Enfield permanent displays and twice-yearly exhibitions.
venues.why_fits_paints: The collection has good local-story material, from mammoth bones and Roman coffin to Enfield industrial objects. It is Tier B because the strongest 2026 community art exhibition evidence found had ended by 13 June 2026.
venues.anecdote_story: Museum of Enfield holds about 20,000 objects relating to Enfield's people, places and businesses.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Museum of Enfield permanent gallery, local-history objects, occasional exhibitions and arts-centre programme.
venues.safety_notes: Central Enfield Town setting; good transit access and safer daytime/evening routing than rural sites.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 5 min walk; cultural/weather fallback, not same-category.
venues.risks_watchouts: Do not make current-exhibition claims without a fresh Dugdale listing.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Chain bookshop and no verified independent coffee/book-cafe identity; fails PAINTS independent Books & Coffee rule.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Pickled Pepper Books pop-up at Culture Palace / Enfield Town
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Promising historical bookshop/culture-cafe signal, but evidence found relates to a past pop-up/collaboration rather than a current operating Enfield venue.
Excellent future PAINTS candidate, but the museum opens to the public on 21 July 2026, after the 4 July 2026 run date; cafe only is not enough for Museum & Gallery Days.
Not on the PAINTS approved Creative Walk safety list; cannot source even though it is locally prominent.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Lee Valley Regional Park extended routes
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: PAINTS v6 explicitly removes Lee Valley Regional Park for Creative Walk routing; Myddelton House can be used as a garden/museum venue but not as an extended Lee Valley walk.
Wetherspoons chain pub; fails PAINTS independent/character operator rule.
Chain pub/bar with weak PAINTS story and aesthetic fit; rejected despite central location.
Handsome local pub but operated by Young's and less distinctive than Little Green Dragon/Wonder for PAINTS; could be emergency backup only.
Ember Inns/Mitchells & Butlers chain format; sufficient capacity but too templated for PAINTS.
